rulings.Case Title: PORTSIDE MASTER HOMEOWNERS' ASSOCIATION VS. PORTSIDE INVESTMENTS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Further Written Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday September 12, 2013 Line 2 - DEFENDANT PORTSIDE INVESTMENTS, LLC, and SF OAKLAND BAY, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Written Responses. Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted in part. Parties are to appear for argument and submit proposed Orders. The Court tentatively will require plaintiff Portside Master Owners¿ Association to provide a privilege log for all redacted information that is based on an attorney-client and/or attorney-work product. With regard to responses where plaintiffs state they have an inability to comply, the plaintiff shall supplement the response with a statement setting forth the name and address of any natural person or organization known or believed by plaintiff to have possession custody or control of that item or category. Any other Orders will be determined after argument. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: YVONNE EASHMAN-SEWELL VS. CRAIG K MARTIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2013, Line 3, CROSS DEFENDANT JUDY EASHMAN-BOWERS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-Defendant Judy Eashman-Bowers' demurrer to Cross-Complainant Martin's First Amended Complaint is granted with 10 days leave to amend as to the third cause of action for fraud, and overruled as to the second, fourth, and fifth causes of action. Martin's request for judicial notice is granted. As to the third cause of action for fraud, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend for Martin to allege facts, if Cross-Complainant can do so in good faith, with sufficient specificity as to moving Cross-Defendant. (See Stansfield v. Starkey (1990) 220 Cal. App. 3d 59, 73 ("particularity requirement necessitates pleading facts which 'show how, when, where, to whom and by what means the representations were tendered'") (emphasis in original).) The demurrers as to the second, fourth, and fifth causes of action are overruled; the issues raised in the demurrer are factual disputes that cannot be determined on demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CATHAY BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. ERIC CHUNG AN INDIVIDUAL AKA ERIC KINCHIU CHUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order Of Entry Of Judgmnt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2013, Line 5, PLAINTIFF CATHAY BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION's Motion For Order Of Entry Of Judgment. Denied without prejudice. Plaintiff did not comply with the terms set forth in the Stipulation for Entry of Judgment ("stipulation"). Per the stipulation, "[i]f a payment is in default, Plaintiff shall mail and fax a letter to Defendants giving them 10 days from the date of mailing to cure the default." (Jenkins Decl., Exhibit 1, p. 2:14-15.) In his declaration, Plaintiff's counsel states that on August 2, 2013, he mailed the letter to Defendants. (Jenkins Decl., 6.) However, Plaintiff's counsel does not state that he faxed the letter of default. Plaintiff has not provided any argument or evidence why it should be excused from complying with the terms of the stipulation. Further, Plaintiff seeks attorney fees of $ 18,916.24, inclusive of default collection efforts, which exceed the stipulated amount of $ 7,100.00. (Compare Jenkins Decl., p. 8:6-12; with id. at Exhibit 1, 1.) Under the stipulation, upon default, Defendants agreed that judgment may be entered pursuant to Stipulation, 1, less the total of all payments made by Defendants. (Jenkins Decl., Exhibit 1, 4.) The Court cannot determine whether Plaintiff's calculations are correct. Plaintiff has not shown its calculations of how each of the 12 payments of $ 5,000 was applied to the reduction of principal and the payment of accrued interest. Plaintiff has not identified the rate of interest charged every month or the accrued interest based on the rate of prime plus 3.25 % (with a floor of 6.00%). (Id. at p. 1:24-25.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ROSA MARIA BUZON et al VS. ALEXANDER ROBERT VARTAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Discovery; Req. For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT ALEXANDER VARTAN's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery; Request For Sanctions. Granted. Rosa Buzon and Lee Nathan Buzon shall serve verified responses, without objection, to Defendant's Form Interrogatories (Set One), Specially Prepared Interrogatories (Set One), and Demand for Identification and Inspection for Documents (Set One) and provide for inspection of all responsive documents no later than September 18, 2013. All objections are waived for failure to timely serve responses. (CCP §§ 2030.290(a); 2031.300(a).) Monetary sanctions are issued in favor of Defendants and against Plaintiffs only, and not their counsel, in the amount of $ 572.00 to be paid within 30 days of this order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: FRESCO MUSIC, LLC VS. ALJOY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Deft'S Verified Responses To Written Disco
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday September 12, 2013 Line 5 - PLAINTIFFs FRESCO MUSIC, LLC, JACOBO JUAREZ and NHU TRUONG's Motion To Compel Deft'S Verified Responses To Written Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to compel verifications is granted in part. C.C.P. 2030.220 requires an individual to provide verifications based on his/her personal knowledge. Mr. Glikshtern shall provide the proper verifications to the discovery at issue. The motion to compel is denied as to defendant Aljoy, Inc. as the issue is moot. The request for sanctions is denied as the Court finds substantial justification in providing the verifications late. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: IBRAHIM MOHAMMED VS. LEADING INSURANCE SERVICES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Compl
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2013, Line 14, PLAINTIFF IBRAHIM MOHAMMED's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint. Granted, in part. Within 10 days, Plaintiff may file his First Amended Complaint. Defendant does not oppose the motion as a matter of legal principle. (Opposition, p. 1:24-25.) To the extent that Defendant qualifies its stipulation by opposing some of the proposed allegations, Defendant may file a demurrer or other motion to challenge the allegations of the First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff's request that the proposed First Amended Complaint be deemed filed and served upon the granting of the instant motion is denied. Plaintiff must actually file and serve the First Amended Complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, ALSO SUED HEREIN AS CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION (AKA CALTRANS OR THE STATE) DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained, with leave to amend. Plaintiff cannot allege a negligence cause of action against Defendant Caltrans. "In California all government tort liability is now dependent on statute." (Susman v. City of Los Angeles (1969) 269 Cal.App.2d 803, 808.) "Under the [Government Claims] Act, there is no common law liability of a public entity; liability is wholly statutory." (4 Witkin, Cal. Proc. (5th ed. 2008) Plead, § 617, p. 748; see Gov. Code § 815.) Statutory causes of action must be specifically pled, and every element of the statutory basis for liability must be specially alleged. (Ibid.) Plaintiff is granted 10 days leave to amend, if she can do so in good faith, to specially plead a statutory cause of action against Defendant Caltrans. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
